questions.question: 1 (a) Ensure that the metre rule is horizontal. You may need to adjust the height of one of the clamps slightly. Briefly describe how you check that the metre rule is horizontal. G present and triangle method seen on graph (timing) errors have less effect / smaller % uncertainty repeat each reading and calculate average value θ for cup A decreasing θ for cup B decreasing and recorded to at least 1 °C decreasing more slowly than θ for cup A

correct variable resistor symbol (rectangle with strike-through arrow only) in completed series circuit 0625/52 March 2021 4 MP1 factor: named factor MP2 method: measure time for motion of ball and means of doing so (stopwatch / timer) over measured distance and means of measuring (probably metre rule / tape measure) / mention of between fixed points MP3 repeat for new value of the independent variable MP4 control: any variable appropriate to independent variable e.g. mass of ball if diameter is facto r MP5 table: columns, with units, at least for independent variable, time MP6 analysis: compare readings in the table to see if change in factor produces change in speed, plot line graph (with axes specified) MP7 additional point (one from) : at least 5 sets of data taken, repeat each measurement and take average, repeat (whole) experiment for same factor but a new condition use of fiducial aid (e.g. mark fixed points to time between) release ball without pushing suitable means of release
